title: Innovative Intensely Kenyan Beef Tacos
description: A sour and aerated Kenyan dish featuring tortillas and braise, this crowd-pleaser is low-fat-friendly and perfect for any occasion. The combination of flavors and textures will leave you wanting more.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat.
Add the beef brisket and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, ginger, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add 2 cups of water and bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 2 hours.
After 2 hours, add the sukuma wiki and lemon juice, and stir to combine.
Continue to simmer for another 30 minutes, or until the beef is tender and the sukuma wiki is wilted.
Warm the tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the tacos by spooning the beef and sukuma wiki mixture onto a tortilla and serving immediately.
